name: Razorpay Payment Gateway — Bulkhead Isolation
scenario: Razorpay's payment service calls 4 downstream APIs (HDFC, VISA, Aadhaar KYC, FraudDetection). During an HDFC NEFT outage, all 200 Tomcat threads get consumed by 10-second HDFC timeouts, causing VISA/KYC/Fraud calls to also fail. Implement Resilience4j Bulkhead isolation so HDFC failures never impact other gateways.

core.description: BulkheadConfiguration @Configuration: 4 Bulkhead beans (hdfc=50, visa=40, kyc=30, fraud=20 concurrent calls, 100ms wait). BulkheadRegistry.

PaymentGatewayService @Service: callHdfc(), callVisa(), callKyc(), callFraud() — each guarded by its respective Bulkhead (manual tryAcquirePermission/onComplete in finally). Each has a typed fallback method returning {status: FALLBACK, gateway: NAME}.
GatewayRouter @Service: route(merchantId, preferredGateway) — tries preferred first, cascades to VISA fallback, then HDFC fallback, then ultimate fallback response.
Micrometer: bulkhead.available_calls Gauge per gateway (tag: name), bulkhead.rejected_calls Counter per gateway (tag: name). Incremented correctly.
@SpringBootTest (5 tests): normal HDFC call succeeds; HDFC bulkhead full → VISA cascade; VISA also full → fallback message; rejected counter increments; available gauge reads correctly. All 5 pass.
README: Little's Law sizing calculation for each gateway (throughput × latency = concurrent calls) and why +20% buffer.

bonus.description: ThreadPoolBulkhead for HDFC (coreSize=20, maxSize=40, queue=10). callHdfcAsync() returns CompletableFuture<GatewayResponse>. Test: result correct.
@CircuitBreaker + @Bulkhead combination on HDFC method. Multiple fallback overloads: BulkheadFullException, CallNotPermittedException, generic Exception. Test: each fallback type triggered.
K8s Pod YAML: payment-service + fluent-bit sidecar + envoy-proxy sidecar. emptyDir shared volume. Comment explaining shared localhost network namespace.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. tryAcquirePermission() + onComplete() in finally: always release the permit even if exception thrown — otherwise the slot is permanently consumed and the bulkhead gradually exhausts itself. maxWaitDuration: how long to wait for a permit before throwing BulkheadFullException — set to 0ms for immediate rejection (better for payment APIs than waiting). Gauge registration in constructor: register once, not on every call — Gauge reads the current value at scrape time. Fallback method signature: same return type, same parameters, plus the exception type as last parameter. Spring picks the most specific exception type. Little's Law: concurrent_calls = throughput_per_second × average_latency_seconds — size your bulkhead to this + 20% buffer.
